targetProfile.intendedUse: Internal scientific review of mercury food-contaminant evidence with explicit methylmercury and inorganic-mercury dietary provenance.
notes: This profile supports review-oriented mercury source lookup and documentation without implying a native mercury submission engine.

referencedResources.description: Operator guide for governed dossier readiness and review workflows.
Validation framework covering governed readiness and follow-up workflows.
Governed readiness rules applied to review dossier assessments.
Governed readiness profiles for adapter and contaminant-family dossier review.
Workflow guide for contaminant monitoring readiness, signoff, and dossier export.
Reviewer signoff guidance for contaminant monitoring action items and waivers.
Governed source database documentation for contaminant monitoring evidence and family posture.
Manifest for governed contaminant legal-limit lookup records referenced by readiness-side legal-limit support reviews.
Manifest for jurisdiction coverage posture referenced by readiness-side legal-limit support reviews.
Governed contaminant legal-limit records for the mercury_food_contaminants family referenced by readiness-side legal-limit support reviews.
Jurisdiction-scoped governed contaminant legal-limit records for eu referenced by readiness-side legal-limit support reviews.
Jurisdiction coverage posture for eu referenced by readiness-side legal-limit support reviews.
Operator guide for the readiness-side queue bundle used as the source of truth for this board.
Operator guide for reviewer-operable routing of scientific follow-up queues.
Operator guide for owner-lane handoff packets derived from the scientific follow-up review board.
Operator guide for owner-lane remediation packets derived from scientific follow-up handoffs.
Operator guide for owner-lane signoff packets derived from scientific follow-up remediation packets.

notes: Owner signoff packet is a reviewer-facing overlay on top of the owner remediation packet and does not mutate readiness, queue, board, handoff, or dossier state.
Waived actions remain explicitly visible so downstream reviewers can inspect accepted deviations from the default owner remediation sequence.
Signed-off status does not imply submission readiness, regulatory approval, or closure of the underlying scientific issue outside the tracked owner lane.
Legal-limit support reviews remain attached to the owner signoff packet so signoff cannot be mistaken for exact jurisdiction coverage when support is partial, anchor-only, or absent.
